questions.question: Explain the primary security limitation of an SPV (Simplified Payment Verification) client compared to a full node. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: SPV nodes do not validate all consensus rules
They rely on full nodes to provide them with data (Merkle proofs)
This makes them vulnerable to certain attacks (e.g., blockchain forks, spoofed nodes) that full nodes would inherently reject

questions.question: Describe the process of how a transaction is created, signed, and broadcast to the network from a non-custodial HD wallet. Additionally, discuss the critical importance of the seed phrase for backup and recovery. (10 marks)
expectedPoints: Transaction Creation: User specifies amount and recipient address. Wallet software constructs an unsigned transaction using Unspent Transaction Outputs (UTXOs).
Signing: The wallet uses the private key(s), derived from the HD seed, corresponding to the UTXOs to create a digital signature for the transaction.
Broadcasting: The signed transaction is sent to connected peer nodes on the Bitcoin P2P network.
HD Wallet Explanation: An HD wallet uses a single master seed to generate a hierarchy of keys.
Seed Phrase Role: The seed phrase is a human-readable representation of this master seed.
Backup Importance: Writing down and securing the seed phrase allows for the recovery of all generated private keys and funds in the wallet if the device is lost or damaged.
Warning: Anyone with access to the seed phrase gains control over all derived funds.
